MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for executor. Return bid is $432,000.00. Bid is increased to $453,050.00. Proceeds will be placed into a blocked account. Verified Second Amended Petn. For Order Setting Aside Loan Transaction & Canceling & Declaring Void Promissory Note & Deed Of Trust Secured By Prop. Of The Estate Without Court Permission, Caused By Lender'S Fraudulent Inducement Of Executor'S Direct Self-Dealing With Prop. Prop. Purportedly For Executor'S Own Benefit
Off Calendar May-11-2006 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Executor'S Petition Due To Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jun-06-2006 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for La Rell Franklin, executor. David Gonden specially appearing for respondents Accredited Home Lenders and Countrywide Home Loan on Motion to dismiss petition and specially appearing for all of the respondents on Motion for sanctions. Counsel received the tentative ruling. Mr. Canatella argues the jurisdiction issue. Court finds the probate court lacks subject matter jurisdiction. Motion is granted with prejudice to filing in the probate court a petition of these same facts. The 2nd amended petition to set aside loan transaction is dismissed. Court wants to modify the language in the order. Mr. Gonden will submit a new order. Motion for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Leticia Perez. Clerk: Ella Yip.
